calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jan-02-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Will Doyle for Dorothy Benson (Petitioner).

The Court stated that the Spouse's declaration seems to suggest they intentionally chose joint tenancy in order to have transfer. Mr. Doyle spoke of the couples lack of knowledge as to title. The Petition for Spousal Property is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Dec-05-2017 Continued To Dec-19-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Oct-31-2017 Continued To Dec-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Will Doyle for Dorothy Benson (Petitioner).

Mr. Doyle presented oral argument to support his position stating that joint earnings and joint savings during the course of the marriage was used to purchase the property, and spoke of the case he cited (Marriage of Valli) and its applicability. The Court noted that Valli was a dissolution case and speaks of actions between spouses, and questions whether the purpose of receiving a full step up in basis is an appropriate use of a spousal property petition. The Petition for Spousal Property is continued to December 5, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to provide supplemental information/evidence.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Oct-10-2017 Continued To Oct-31-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
